title: Neottia orchioides
taxonomy: Neottia orchioides is classified in the kingdom Plantae, with its specific classification being Neottia orchioides, a species within the family Orchidaceae. This plant belongs to the order Asparagales, which is a group of flowering plants. Further classification places it within the family Orchidaceae, specifically within the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Neottieae. Within its tribe, Neottia orchioides is a member of the genus Neottia, which comprises terrestrial orchids with a unique growth habit. Neottia orchioides is commonly known as the Yellow Bird's Nest or Yellow Bird Orchid.

intro: Neottia orchioides, also known as the Chinese grass pink or orchid-leafed neottia, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Neottia, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Neottia orchioides include alkaloids, phenylethanoid glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Neottia orchioides is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used in herbal preparations such as cough syrups and anti-inflammatory ointments.
